summary.intent: Implement CHA-35: cron registration for a follow-up engine that periodically queries the CRM for stale contacts and fires an agent turn to notify the user, mirroring the existing heartbeat job pattern.
summary.outcome: The implementation was already largely in place on the branch; the agent fixed two rustfmt formatting issues in gateway/src/lib.rs and gateway/src/server.rs, confirmed clean compilation, and was waiting on test results at the end of the session.

repo: Heartbeat job registration (server.rs:4925-5035) is the canonical pattern for system cron jobs — upsert with fixed ID, config-driven enable/disable.

CRM PII fields are wrapped in Secret<String>; follow-up prompts must use a StaleContactSummary struct (name, stage, last_interaction_date, matter_title) and never expose raw Contact objects to the LLM.
The data retention engine uses a detached tokio::spawn loop (not a cron job), which is considered an anti-pattern relative to the CronService approach.
The moltis-cron crate supports both CronSchedule::Every { every_ms } (heartbeat) and CronSchedule::Cron { expr } (calendar-aware, used for follow-up).
CRM stale contact queries must use a dedicated store method (contacts_needing_followup) with a LEFT JOIN rather than loading all contacts and filtering in Rust.
Session target detection uses SessionTarget::Named("follow_up") in the on_agent_turn callback, sharing a const to avoid typos.
DRY extraction of a system job helper is intentionally deferred until a third system job appears (two instances not enough to justify abstraction).

code.finding: Heartbeat job registration block — exact pattern to mirror for follow-up job upsert.
Required rustfmt fixes — module declaration formatting was non-conformant.
Required rustfmt fixes — follow-up registration block had formatting issues.

workflow: cargo check was run excluding the web crate (which requires a CSS build step) to avoid spurious failures.
rustfmt --check with nightly toolchain is part of the quality gate and must pass before tests.
The agent used TaskOutput polling to retrieve async cargo check and test results rather than waiting synchronously.
Linear comments serve as a persistent workpad — engineering review decisions and CEO plan review are both stored there and read before implementation begins.

friction: cargo check took long enough to require async polling via TaskOutput, blocking progress visibility.
rustfmt required two rounds of fixes — initial edits to lib.rs and server.rs did not pass the fmt check on the first attempt, requiring re-reading and re-editing both files.
Test results were not yet available at session end — the session ended while waiting on TaskOutput for the test run.
open_items: Stage-aware follow-up thresholds (per-stage stale_days config) — deferred to a future PR.
Autonomous outreach capability — deferred pending product/legal decision.
Follow-up outcome tracking (new CRM interaction types) — deferred.
FOLLOW_UP.md prompt file (mirroring HEARTBEAT.md) — nice-to-have, not implemented.
Retention engine migration from tokio::spawn to CronService — out of scope, separate concern.
Extraction of a register_system_job() helper in server.rs — deferred until a third system job is added.
Potential future system_jobs.rs module extraction as more system cron jobs accumulate in server.rs startup block.
Test results were not confirmed at session end — test suite pass/fail status unknown.
